project.name: Boonz ERP
project.description: Smart vending operations platform for UAE — management ERP, field staff PWA, client portal, and agentic command layer

stack.frontend: Next.js 14 (App Router), TypeScript strict, Tailwind CSS
stack.backend: Supabase — Postgres + Auth + RLS + Realtime + Storage

open_questions.question: Actual company name for supplier SUP_012 — currently contact person name used as fallback
How to populate machine_product_pricing initially — manual entry or script from sales_history price variance?
Enrichment strategy for sales_history.boonz_product_id NULLs (10,332 rows)
Backfill strategy for sales_history.machine_id NULLs (3,445 rows) — machine_name_aliases coverage unknown
VPS hosting — n8n + FastAPI on same instance or separate?
Which Telegram user IDs go in TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS whitelist?
Default client alert thresholds — stock % and revenue floor — can differ per client
22 Feb 2026 Adyen/POS reconciliation discrepancy — root cause investigation needed before Phase 3 sign-off

manager.scope: all modules except billing + user config

field_staff.scope: their trips, pod check-ins, refill confirmation

db_conventions.note: All table and column names below are exact Supabase Postgres names. Use these verbatim in queries.
db_conventions.price_lookup: Always query machine_product_pricing first (filter is_active=true, machine_id, boonz_product_id). Fall back to product_pricing.default_price_aed if no row found. Use view v_current_price for UI queries.
db_conventions.sales_history: Append-only table — never UPDATE or DELETE rows.
db_conventions.adyen_join: Reconcile adyen_transactions to sales_history on: store (adyen) = machine mapping key + date + captured_amount_value = total_amount. Exclude rows where creation_date < 2026-02-06 (test transactions).
db_conventions.machine_id_nulls: sales_history.machine_id is NULL for 3,445 rows due to renaming history. Resolve via machine_name_aliases (original_name → official_name → machines.machine_id). Do not write resolution logic into UI — it belongs in a data enrichment job.
db_conventions.product_id_nulls: sales_history.boonz_product_id is NULL for 10,332 rows. Do not block UI features on this — show 'Unknown product' in UI where product_id is NULL.
db_conventions.idempotency: All field staff writes must be idempotent. Use ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING or upsert with composite unique keys.

phases.name: Foundation
phases.summary: Next.js scaffold, Supabase auth, 7-role system, route groups, CI/CD

stories.title: Scaffold Next.js 14 monorepo with 4 route groups
stories.story: As a developer, I want the Next.js app scaffolded with 4 route groups so that each surface (ERP, field, portal, chat) has isolated layouts and middleware.
tasks: Run: npx create-next-app@latest boonz-erp --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir --import-alias '@/*'
Create route group folders: src/app/(app)/, src/app/(field)/, src/app/(portal)/, src/app/(chat)/
Each group gets layout.tsx (empty shell) and page.tsx returning a placeholder <div>
Create src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx as a placeholder
Add .gitignore entries: .env.local, .env*.local, node_modules/
acceptance_criteria: npx next build completes with 0 errors
Visiting / in browser renders without errors
4 route groups exist in src/app/

stories.title: Install Supabase client libraries and set up env vars
stories.story: As a developer, I want Supabase clients configured for browser and server so that components can query the DB without boilerplate.
tasks: Run: npm install @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/ssr
Create .env.local with: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
Create src/lib/supabase/client.ts — browser client using createBrowserClient from @supabase/ssr
Create src/lib/supabase/server.ts — server client using createServerClient with cookie handling
Create src/lib/supabase/middleware.ts — session refresh helper that calls supabase.auth.getUser()
acceptance_criteria: Import { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase/client' works in a client component
Import { createClient } from '@/lib/supabase/server' works in a server component
SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY is never imported in any file under src/app/ directly — only via server.ts or Edge Functions
env_vars: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY=<from Supabase dashboard → Settings → API — never expose to client>

stories.title: Create user_profiles table with RLS
stories.story: As a developer, I want a user_profiles table linked to auth.users so that roles and operator/client scoping are stored and enforced at DB level.
tasks: Apply migration: create user_profiles table (see sql below)
Enable RLS on user_profiles
Create policy 'users_own_profile': SELECT where auth.uid() = id
Create policy 'admins_manage_profiles': ALL where role IN ('superadmin','operator_admin') for the calling user
Create trigger: on INSERT to auth.users → insert a row into user_profiles with default role='field_staff'
acceptance_criteria: user_profiles table exists with RLS enabled
A user with role='client' cannot SELECT rows where id != their own auth.uid()
A user with role='superadmin' can SELECT all rows
Migration committed to supabase/migrations/

stories.title: Auth middleware — session refresh and role-based routing
stories.story: As a user, I want to be automatically redirected to my correct surface after login so I never land on a screen that isn't mine.
tasks: Create src/middleware.ts at project root
On every request: call supabase.auth.getUser() to refresh session
If no session: redirect to /login (except /login itself)
After session confirmed: read user_profiles.role for the authenticated user
Redirect rules: field_staff → /field, warehouse → /field, client → /portal, all others → /app
Exclude from middleware: /login, /_next/, /favicon.ico, /api/ routes
acceptance_criteria: Unauthenticated request to /app redirects to /login
Authenticated field_staff user visiting /app is redirected to /field
Authenticated client user visiting /app is redirected to /portal
Authenticated manager visiting /field is redirected to /app

stories.title: Login page — email + password
stories.story: As an operator admin, I want a login page so I can authenticate and access the ERP.
tasks: Build src/app/(auth)/login/page.tsx as a client component
Email input + password input + submit button
On submit: call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword({ email, password })
On success: router.push('/app') — middleware then re-routes per role
On error: show inline error message below the form (not toast)
Add 'Forgot password?' link that calls supabase.auth.resetPasswordForEmail(email)
No OAuth / social login in Phase 1
acceptance_criteria: Valid credentials → user is redirected to their role-appropriate surface
Invalid credentials → inline error appears: 'Invalid email or password'
Forgot password link triggers Supabase reset email (verify in Supabase Auth logs)
Page renders correctly at 375px viewport width (no horizontal scroll)

stories.title: Management sidebar layout shell
stories.story: As a manager, I want a sidebar navigation so I can move between ERP modules.
tasks: Build src/app/(app)/layout.tsx with a collapsible sidebar
Nav items (all placeholders in P1): Dashboard, Pods, Refill & Dispatch, Products, Inventory, Financials, Suppliers, Consumers, Settings
Show/hide nav items based on role from user_profiles — finance role hides Pods, Refill, Consumers; manager hides Settings and Financials billing
Active route highlighted using usePathname()
Sidebar collapses to icon-only on mobile (< 768px)
acceptance_criteria: Sidebar renders with all 9 nav items for operator_admin
Finance role user does not see Pods, Refill & Dispatch, or Consumers nav items
Active route item is visually highlighted
Sidebar collapses to icons below 768px

stories.title: Field PWA bottom nav layout shell
stories.story: As a driver, I want a bottom navigation bar so I can switch between my trips, pods, and inventory views.
tasks: Build src/app/(field)/layout.tsx with a fixed bottom tab bar
4 tabs: Trips, Pods, Inventory, Profile
Each tab is a link — active tab highlighted
All tab targets minimum 44px height (accessibility)
Navigation works without network (client-side routing only)
acceptance_criteria: Bottom nav renders with 4 tabs
Each tab target is at least 44px tall
Active tab is visually distinct
Switching tabs does not trigger a network request

stories.title: CI/CD — GitHub repo + Vercel auto-deploy
stories.story: As a developer, I want every push to main auto-deployed to Vercel so I never ship manually.
tasks: Create GitHub repo: boonz-erp (private)
Connect repo to Vercel — auto-deploy main branch to production
Create dev branch — Vercel creates preview URL automatically
Add branch protection on main: require PR + at least 1 passing check
Create supabase/migrations/ folder — commit initial migration files
Add NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY as Vercel environment variables (Production + Preview)
Add SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY as Vercel environment variable (Production only — never Preview)
acceptance_criteria: Push to main triggers Vercel production deploy automatically
Push to dev creates a Vercel preview URL
Direct push to main is blocked — PR required
supabase/migrations/ folder exists with at least user_profiles migration file

phases.name: Field Staff PWA
phases.summary: Driver trip flow + warehouse receiving/dispatch — replaces AppSheet

stories.title: Driver — today's trip stop list
stories.story: As a driver, I want to see my ordered stop list for today so I know exactly where to go.
tasks: Query refill_dispatching WHERE dispatch_date = today AND dispatched = false, join machines on machine_id
Group by machine_id — each machine is one stop
Order stops by machines.pod_location (alphabetical in Phase 2 — routing optimisation is Phase 7+)
Display: stop number, machine official_name, pod_location, pod_address, number of SKU lines for that stop
Cache result in IndexedDB (key: trip_date) for offline access
Pull-to-refresh to sync latest from Supabase
acceptance_criteria: Driver sees list of today's machines with stop numbers
List loads from IndexedDB when offline
Pull-to-refresh fetches latest from Supabase when online
Tapping a stop navigates to stop detail screen (P2-S2)

stories.title: Driver — stop detail and check-in
stories.story: As a driver, I want to check in at a machine with one tap and have my GPS location logged so there's no paperwork.
tasks: Stop detail screen: machine name, address, list of refill lines (product name, planned quantity)
CHECK IN button: call navigator.geolocation.getCurrentPosition(), capture lat/lng + timestamp
Write check-in to a new table trip_events: { machine_id, driver_user_id, event_type='check_in', latitude, longitude, timestamp }
If GPS differs from expected location by > 200m: show warning 'You appear to be at a different location' — driver can proceed (not blocked)
After check-in: show refill confirmation form (P2-S3)
Check-in write must be idempotent: ON CONFLICT (machine_id, driver_user_id, dispatch_date, event_type='check_in') DO NOTHING
acceptance_criteria: Check-in button writes a trip_events row within 2 seconds when online
Check-in is queued in IndexedDB when offline and syncs on reconnect
GPS warning appears when distance > 200m — driver is not blocked
Duplicate check-in tap does not create duplicate rows

db_tables_written.note: New table — create migration. Columns: id uuid PK, machine_id uuid FK machines, driver_user_id uuid FK auth.users, event_type text CHECK IN ('check_in','check_out','issue_reported'), dispatch_date date, latitude numeric, longitude numeric, gps_variance_m numeric, created_at timestamptz

stories.title: Driver — refill confirmation (actual vs planned)
stories.story: As a driver, I want to confirm actual quantities placed per SKU — pre-filled with planned — and only edit what differs.
tasks: Show each refill_dispatching line for this machine: product name, planned quantity pre-filled in input
Driver edits only lines that differ from plan
On submit: for each line, UPDATE refill_dispatching SET filled_quantity = <actual>, item_added = true, dispatched = true WHERE dispatch_id = <id>
Write must be queued in IndexedDB if offline — sync on reconnect
After submit: show expired/damaged logging screen (P2-S4)
acceptance_criteria: Each line shows planned quantity pre-filled
Driver can change quantity for any line
Submit updates all refill_dispatching rows for that machine with filled_quantity and dispatched=true
Offline: confirmation queued, 'Pending sync' indicator visible
Reconnect: queued confirmations sync automatically, user notified of success

stories.title: Driver — expired and damaged product logging
stories.story: As a driver, I want to log expired or damaged products I remove from a machine so inventory stays accurate.
tasks: After refill confirmation: show 'Log removals' screen
Driver can add lines: product (select from pod_products for this machine), quantity, reason (dropdown: 'Expired', 'Damaged', 'Other')
On submit: INSERT into pod_inventory a row with status='Removed' (or 'Removed / Expired') for each logged item
Also UPDATE pod_inventory.current_stock by subtracting removed quantity WHERE machine_id = <id> AND boonz_product_id matches
Screen is skippable — 'Nothing to remove' button
acceptance_criteria: Driver can add one or more removal lines
Each removal creates a pod_inventory row with appropriate status
Skipping logs nothing and proceeds to trip close
Writes queued offline, sync on reconnect

stories.title: Driver — machine issue reporting with photo
stories.story: As a driver, I want to report a machine issue with a photo so the manager is notified immediately.
tasks: Issue report button available from stop detail screen at any time (before or after refill)
Form: issue type (dropdown: 'Hardware', 'Payment terminal', 'Display', 'Other'), description text, photo (optional)
Photo: use <input type='file' accept='image/*' capture='environment'> — compress to < 1MB client-side before upload
Upload photo to Supabase Storage at path: machine-issues/{machine_id}/{timestamp}.jpg
INSERT into machine_issues: { id, machine_id, reporter_user_id, issue_type, description, photo_storage_path, status='open', created_at }
Note: machine_issues is a new table — create migration
Supabase Realtime on machine_issues INSERT → n8n flow notify_manager fires Telegram alert to manager
acceptance_criteria: Driver can submit issue with or without photo
Photo is compressed to < 1MB before upload
Photo URL stored in machine_issues.photo_storage_path
Manager receives Telegram alert within 30 seconds of submission
Issue report works offline: queued and synced on reconnect

db_tables_written.note: New table — migration required. Columns: id uuid PK, machine_id uuid FK machines, reporter_user_id uuid FK auth.users, issue_type text, description text, photo_storage_path text, status text DEFAULT 'open' CHECK('open','in_progress','resolved'), created_at timestamptz, resolved_at timestamptz

stories.title: Warehouse — goods receiving against PO
stories.story: As a warehouse user, I want to receive a delivery by checking it against the PO and entering actual quantities and expiry dates so every batch is traceable.
tasks: Warehouse home: list of purchase_orders with status != 'Received', ordered by purchase_date desc
Receiving screen per PO: show po_id, supplier_name (join suppliers), list of PO lines (boonz_products.boonz_product_name, ordered_qty)
For each line: enter received_qty and expiry_date (date picker)
If received_qty > ordered_qty: show warning 'Quantity exceeds PO — confirm?' with 'Proceed anyway' button — write received_qty to a received_variance field
On submit: UPDATE purchase_orders SET received_date = today WHERE po_line_id = <id>
INSERT into warehouse_inventory: { boonz_product_id, snapshot_date = today, warehouse_stock = received_qty, expiration_date, batch_id = po_id + '-' + timestamp, wh_location, status = 'Active' }
batch_id format: '{po_id}-{unix_timestamp}'
acceptance_criteria: Warehouse user can see open POs
Receiving a PO line creates a warehouse_inventory row with correct batch_id and expiry_date
Overage warning shown when received_qty > ordered_qty
purchase_orders.received_date updated on submit
Manager notified (Telegram) if received_qty differs from ordered_qty by > 10%

stories.title: Warehouse — expiry sweep
stories.story: As a warehouse user, I want to run an expiry sweep filtered by days-to-expiry so nothing expired leaves the warehouse.
tasks: Expiry sweep tab: filter buttons for 0, 3, 15, 30 days-to-expiry
Query warehouse_inventory WHERE status = 'Active' AND expiration_date <= today + N days, join boonz_products for name
Show list: product name, batch_id, wh_location, expiration_date, warehouse_stock quantity
Action per row: 'Remove' button → UPDATE warehouse_inventory SET status = 'Expired', updated_at = now()
acceptance_criteria: Filter buttons correctly scope to items expiring within the selected window
Removing an item updates its status to 'Expired'
Expired items disappear from the 'Active' list after status update
Page refreshes list after each removal action

stories.title: Offline sync engine
stories.story: As a field staff user, I want the app to work offline and sync automatically when I reconnect so I never lose data.
tasks: Implement an offline queue using IndexedDB (library: idb or Dexie.js)
All write operations from P2-S2, P2-S3, P2-S4, P2-S5, P2-S6 must go through the queue
Queue entry schema: { id, table, operation ('insert'|'update'), payload, created_at, synced: false }
On reconnect (navigator.onLine event): flush queue — attempt each write in created_at order
On success: mark queue entry synced=true
On failure: retry 3× with exponential backoff — if all fail, mark status='failed', show user alert
Visual indicator: persistent banner at top showing 'Offline — X actions pending sync' when offline
All writes must be idempotent (see db_conventions.idempotency above)
acceptance_criteria: App functions fully for navigation and read operations while offline
Write actions queued offline appear in Supabase within 10 seconds of reconnecting
No duplicate rows created from offline queue replay
Offline banner visible whenever navigator.onLine === false
Failed syncs shown to user with specific error context

phases.name: Management ERP
phases.summary: 9-module internal platform — operations, pods, refill, products, inventory, financials, suppliers, consumers, settings

stories.title: Operations dashboard — real-time fleet overview
stories.story: As a manager, I want a real-time operations dashboard showing active trips, stock alerts, and machine issues so I stay informed without asking drivers.
tasks: KPI cards row: total active machines, machines with shortage_pct > 30, open issues count, today's dispatched stops
Machine status table: join machines + machine_summary (latest snapshot_date per machine) + open issues count from machine_issues WHERE status='open'
Columns: machine name, location, occupancy %, shortage score, last refill date, open issues
Red highlight rows where shortage_pct > 50
Supabase Realtime subscription on machine_issues — new issue appears in table immediately without page reload
Trip status section: query trip_events grouped by driver_user_id for today — show driver name, stops done / total stops
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard loads in < 2.5 seconds (LCP)
New machine issue appears in table within 3 seconds of driver submission (Realtime)
Machines with shortage_pct > 50 are highlighted red
KPI cards show accurate counts from DB

stories.title: Refill & dispatch — Stage 3 owner review web UI
stories.story: As an operator, I want to review today's refill plan in a web UI — see Claude's reasoning, override quantities, and approve the plan — so I don't have to use the CLI.
tasks: Fetch refill_instructions for today: join machines, shelf_configurations, pod_products
Display as cards grouped by machine. Each card: shelf_code, pod_product_name, current_stock, target_stock, refill_qty (editable input), refill_reason, classification_name
Show change_log.md content as parsed markdown above the cards (read from Supabase Storage: refill-outputs/{date}/change_log.md)
If operator changes refill_qty below a floor (current_stock): show inline warning 'Quantity below current stock — confirm?'
APPROVE button: for each line, UPDATE refill_dispatching SET quantity = <approved_qty>, dispatched = false WHERE machine_id + shelf_id + dispatch_date match
After approve: trigger n8n webhook POST /webhook/refill-trigger with { target_date, approved_by: user_id }
Write approval event to a new table dispatch_approvals: { id, dispatch_date, approved_by, approved_at, override_count, note }
acceptance_criteria: All refill_instructions for today shown, grouped by machine
change_log.md rendered as readable markdown
Operator can edit any refill_qty before approving
Floor violation warning appears when qty < current_stock
Approval updates refill_dispatching quantities and triggers n8n
dispatch_approvals row written on approval

db_tables_written.note: New table — migration required. Columns: id uuid PK, dispatch_date date, approved_by uuid FK auth.users, approved_at timestamptz, override_count int, note text

stories.title: Financials — Adyen + POS reconciliation view
stories.story: As a finance user, I want to see reconciled Adyen and POS transactions with discrepancies clearly flagged so I can produce accurate accounts.
tasks: Reconciliation query: LEFT JOIN adyen_transactions (AT) to sales_history (SH) on AT.store = SH.machine_mapping AND DATE(AT.creation_date) = DATE(SH.transaction_date) AND AT.captured_amount_value = SH.total_amount
Exclude rows where AT.creation_date < '2026-02-06' (test transactions)
Display table columns: date, machine name, Adyen amount (AED), POS amount (AED), payment method, match status
Match status logic: if AT row has matching SH row → 'Matched'; if AT exists but no SH match → 'Adyen only'; if SH exists but no AT match → 'POS only'
Highlight 'Adyen only' and 'POS only' rows in amber; highlight the 22 Feb 2026 discrepancy row in red with a fixed note: 'Known discrepancy — under investigation'
Date range filter (default: last 30 days) and machine filter
Export button: download filtered table as CSV
acceptance_criteria: Matched transactions show on one row with both amounts
Unmatched transactions visible with clear status label
22 Feb 2026 row highlighted red with investigation note
Pre-06 Feb 2026 rows excluded from view
CSV export downloads correctly filtered data
Page loads < 3 seconds for 30-day default range

stories.title: Inventory module — warehouse stock view
stories.story: As a manager, I want to see current warehouse stock levels grouped by product so I know what we have on hand.
tasks: Query warehouse_inventory WHERE status = 'Active', group by boonz_product_id, SUM(warehouse_stock)
Join boonz_products for product name, category
Display: product name, category, total active stock, earliest expiry_date in stock, batch count
Filter by product category (dropdown from distinct values in boonz_products.product_category)
Filter by expiry window: all, expiring in 7 days, expiring in 30 days
Row action: 'View batches' expands to show individual warehouse_inventory rows for that product
acceptance_criteria: Stock totals are accurate SUM of active warehouse_inventory rows
Category filter works correctly
Expiry filter works correctly
Batch expansion shows individual rows with batch_id, wh_location, expiry_date
Products with zero active stock shown with '0' (not hidden)

stories.title: Suppliers module
stories.story: As an operator admin, I want to view and edit supplier records so I can keep contact and contract information current.
tasks: Supplier list: query suppliers ORDER BY supplier_name, show name, category, status, contact_person, contact_email, contract_end_date
Status badge: Active (green), Inactive (grey), Suspended (red)
Supplier detail page: all columns from suppliers table as editable fields
Edit form: UPDATE suppliers WHERE supplier_id = <id>
Note for SUP_012: show a banner 'Company name unknown — please update' if supplier_name matches contact_person (proxy check until OQ-01 resolved)
Read-only section: products supplied (query supplier_product_mapping → pod_products for this supplier)
acceptance_criteria: All 13 suppliers visible in list
Editing a supplier name persists correctly
SUP_012 warning banner visible until supplier_name is updated
Products section shows linked pod_products
Finance role cannot access this module (middleware blocks)

phases.name: Agent Core — Telegram Bot
phases.summary: Telegram bot with Claude tool_use — query, action, and trigger tool classes

stories.title: Telegram webhook handler
stories.story: As a developer, I want a Telegram webhook endpoint that receives messages and routes them to Claude so the bot responds to operator commands.
tasks: Create Supabase Edge Function: supabase/functions/telegram-bot/index.ts
Set webhook with Telegram: POST https://api.telegram.org/bot{TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN}/setWebhook?url={SUPABASE_EDGE_FN_URL}
Verify incoming request using TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN secret (validate X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token header)
Check telegram_user_id against TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_USER_IDS env var — if not in list, respond: 'Not authorised.' and return 200
Load conversation history from agent_sessions table (keyed by chat_id)
Call Claude API with: model='claude-sonnet-4-20250514', system=<agent system prompt>, tools=<all tool definitions>, messages=<history + new message>
Send Claude's text response back to Telegram via sendMessage API
Log tool calls to agent_tool_calls table
Save updated conversation to agent_sessions
acceptance_criteria: Whitelisted Telegram user sends a message → receives Claude's response within 5 seconds
Non-whitelisted user receives 'Not authorised.' only
Conversation history persists across messages in the same chat_id
All tool calls logged to agent_tool_calls with timestamp, tool_name, input, output

db_tables_written.note: New table. Columns: id uuid PK, chat_id text UNIQUE, messages jsonb (array of {role,content}), updated_at timestamptz
New table. Columns: id uuid PK, session_id uuid FK agent_sessions, tool_name text, input jsonb, output jsonb, called_at timestamptz, duration_ms int

stories.title: Agent tool — get_machine_status
stories.story: As an operator, I want to ask 'what's the status of Mercato machines?' and get a plain-language answer from the bot.
tasks: Define Claude tool: name='get_machine_status', input_schema: { machine_id?: string, location?: string }
Implement as Edge Function handler: query machines + machine_summary (latest snapshot per machine) + open issues count
If machine_id provided: return single machine. If location provided: filter by pod_location ILIKE '%{location}%'. If neither: return all
Return: [{ id, official_name, pod_location, status, shortage_pct, last_refill_date (from max dispatch_date in refill_dispatching where dispatched=true), open_issues_count }]
Claude must format this as plain-language response — never expose raw JSON to Telegram user
acceptance_criteria: Tool returns correct machine data from DB
Claude responds in plain language, not JSON
Query 'what's the status of DIFC machines' returns only DIFC machines
Tool call logged to agent_tool_calls

stories.title: Agent tool — get_sales_summary
stories.story: As an operator, I want to ask 'what were sales at Mercato this week?' and get a revenue figure with a breakdown.
tasks: Define Claude tool: name='get_sales_summary', input_schema: { date_from: string (ISO), date_to: string (ISO), machine_ids?: string[], group_by?: 'machine'|'product'|'day' }
Query sales_history WHERE transaction_date BETWEEN date_from AND date_to AND delivery_status = 'Successful'
Join machines for name. Join boonz_products for product name where boonz_product_id is not null
GROUP BY per group_by value, SUM(total_amount) as revenue_aed, COUNT(*) as tx_count
Return: { total_aed, tx_count, rows: [{ group_key, revenue_aed, tx_count }] }
Currency always AED
acceptance_criteria: Tool returns correct totals matching manual DB query
Rows with NULL boonz_product_id counted in total but shown as 'Unknown product' when group_by='product'
date_from and date_to are inclusive
Claude responds with formatted summary: total, top performers, comparison if asked

stories.title: Agent tool — approve_dispatch_plan (action with confirmation)
stories.story: As an operator, I want to approve today's dispatch plan from Telegram without opening a browser.
tasks: Define Claude tool: name='approve_dispatch_plan', input_schema: { date: string, override_notes?: string }

CONFIRMATION REQUIRED before execution: Claude must send a summary message first — 'I am about to approve the dispatch plan for {date} with {N} stops and {M} SKU lines. Reply yes to confirm.' Then wait for user confirmation.
Confirmation expires after 60 seconds — Claude tracks this via conversation context
On confirmed execution: UPDATE refill_dispatching SET dispatched=false WHERE dispatch_date = date (reset to ready-for-dispatch state)
INSERT into dispatch_approvals: { dispatch_date, approved_by, approved_at, note: override_notes }
POST to n8n webhook: N8N_WEBHOOK_BASE_URL + '/webhook/refill-trigger' with { target_date: date, job_id: uuid }
Return: { success: true, approved_at, n8n_trigger_id }
acceptance_criteria: Tool never executes without explicit user confirmation ('yes', 'confirm', 'do it')
Confirmation message shows stop count and SKU line count before execution
60-second expiry: sending 'yes' 70 seconds later gets 'That confirmation has expired' response
Successful approval writes to dispatch_approvals and triggers n8n webhook
n8n webhook call logged to agent_tool_calls with HTTP response code

phases.name: Own GUI Chat
phases.summary: Embedded /chat route in Next.js ERP with streaming, conversation history, context injection

stories.title: Chat UI — streaming response rendering
stories.story: As an operator, I want to chat with the agent inside the ERP and see responses appear word by word so it feels fast.
tasks: Create src/app/(chat)/page.tsx as main chat interface
Message input at bottom, conversation history scrollable above
On send: POST to /api/chat (Next.js API route) which calls Claude API with stream=true
Stream tokens back to client using Server-Sent Events or ReadableStream
Render tokens as they arrive — no waiting for full response
Show typing indicator (3 dots) while waiting for first token
Tool call indicator: when Claude calls a tool, show 'Checking database…' inline
acceptance_criteria: First token appears within 2 seconds of sending message
Tokens render progressively — not all at once
Tool call indicator visible during DB lookups
Conversation history visible and scrollable
Cmd/Ctrl+K from anywhere in /app opens /chat

stories.title: Chat — conversation persistence
stories.story: As an operator, I want my chat history to persist so I can continue a conversation after refreshing the page.
tasks: On page load: query agent_sessions WHERE chat_id = user_id — restore conversation
After each exchange: UPDATE agent_sessions.messages with full conversation array
Show last 50 messages in UI (truncate older for display only — full history sent to Claude for context up to 20 turns)
Clear conversation button: DELETE agent_sessions row for this user_id + clear UI
acceptance_criteria: Refresh page → conversation history restored from DB
Clear button wipes conversation in UI and DB
Max 20 turns sent to Claude API per request (older truncated in API call, kept in DB)

phases.name: Client Portal
phases.summary: Magic link auth, RLS-isolated dashboard, daily PDF reports, alert preferences

stories.title: Client portal — magic link authentication
stories.story: As a client, I want to log in with a magic link so I have no password to remember.
tasks: Create src/app/(portal)/login/page.tsx — email input only, 'Send magic link' button
On submit: supabase.auth.signInWithOtp({ email }) — Supabase sends magic link email
On click (magic link): Supabase redirects to /portal/auth/callback — exchange token, create session
After session: middleware reads user_profiles.role — if not 'client', redirect to /login with error
RLS isolation: all portal queries must include user_profiles.client_id in WHERE clause — enforced at RLS policy level, not just UI
Confirm: a client user cannot access /app or /field (middleware blocks)
acceptance_criteria: Client receives magic link email within 60 seconds
Clicking magic link creates authenticated session and redirects to portal dashboard
Client user cannot access /app — gets redirected to /login
RLS test: client A cannot query machines belonging to client B (Supabase row-level test)

stories.title: Client portal — machine dashboard
stories.story: As a client, I want to see my machines' current stock, recent revenue, and any active issues so I have full visibility.
tasks: Query machines WHERE client_id = user_profiles.client_id (RLS enforces this automatically)
For each machine: show official_name, pod_location, shortage_pct (from latest machine_summary), open_issues count
Revenue card: SUM(total_amount) from sales_history for last 7 days for this client's machines
Top products table: GROUP BY pod_product_name, SUM(total_amount) DESC LIMIT 5, for last 30 days
Supabase Realtime on machine_issues for client's machine_ids — new issues appear without reload
Note: machines table needs client_id column added — migration required
acceptance_criteria: Client sees only their machines — no others visible in any query
Revenue and top products are accurate
New issue appears within 3 seconds via Realtime
Dashboard loads < 2.5 seconds
migrations_needed: ALTER TABLE machines ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS client_id uuid REFERENCES auth.users(id)

stories.title: Daily PDF report — auto-generated and emailed
stories.story: As a client, I want a daily PDF report emailed to me every morning with my machines' revenue and alerts from the previous day.
tasks: n8n flow: daily_client_report cron trigger at 08:00 GST (UTC+4)
For each active client: query previous day's sales_history + machine_issues for their machines
Generate PDF: header (Boonz logo, client name, date), revenue total, transaction count, top 5 products table, issues list
Upload PDF to Supabase Storage at: reports/{client_id}/{YYYY-MM-DD}.pdf
Send email via Resend/Postmark with PDF attached
Store report metadata in client_reports table: { id, client_id, report_date, storage_path, emailed_at }
Retention: Storage lifecycle rule to delete reports older than 90 days
Note: client_reports is a new table — migration required
acceptance_criteria: Report emails arrive by 08:15 GST each day
PDF contains correct data for previous calendar day only
PDF saved to Storage at correct path
client_reports row inserted for each sent report
Reports older than 90 days are deleted automatically

db_tables_written.note: New table. Columns: id uuid PK, client_id uuid FK auth.users, report_date date, storage_path text, emailed_at timestamptz, created_at timestamptz
